evals.name: Write an integration test for a POST endpoint
evals.prompt: Write an integration test for a POST /appointments endpoint in our async FastAPI + Postgres app. It should seed a parent row, call the endpoint, and verify the row was persisted. Use our existing test fixtures and the transactional-rollback isolation.
evals.expected_output: An async test function that uses the `client` and `db_conn` fixtures, seeds via a factory, POSTs through the AsyncClient, asserts on the response envelope, and reads back the row via db_conn within the same transaction (no commit, no manual cleanup).

assertions.description: Test is written as an async def.
Uses the client and db_conn fixtures rather than rolling its own.
Drives the endpoint through the async httpx client.
Reads back DB state via the pinned connection inside the same transaction.
Does NOT use the legacy sync TestClient.
Does NOT mock the database.
Relies on rollback isolation; no explicit commit or manual TRUNCATE.

evals.name: Test an SSE streaming endpoint including the terminal error event
evals.prompt: Write end-to-end tests for our /chat SSE streaming endpoint. Cover the happy path (token events ending in end) and the terminal error case where the stream emits event: error as the last event while the HTTP status stays 200. Fake the LLM; don't hit the network.
evals.expected_output: Async tests that program the fake LLM, open client.stream(...), parse events with the read_sse_events helper, assert the happy path ends with `end`, and assert the failure path's last event is `error` with status still 200.

assertions.description: Opens a streaming request rather than reading .text.
Uses the read_sse_events helper instead of hand-rolling SSE parsing.
Programs the fake LLM at the boundary.
Asserts on the terminal error event.
Confirms the SSE error case keeps HTTP 200.
Does NOT mock the LLM over HTTP.

evals.name: Review a test with three anti-patterns
evals.prompt:
Review this test and tell me what's wrong with it:

```python
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient
from unittest.mock import MagicMock, patch
import respx
from api.main import app

client = TestClient(app)

@respx.mock
def test_chat(monkeypatch):
    respx.post('https://api.anthropic.com/v1/messages').respond(json={'content': [{'type': 'text', 'text': 'hi'}]})
    fake_pool = MagicMock()
    fake_pool.acquire.return_value.fetchrow.return_value = {'id': 1}
    with patch('api.deps.get_pool', return_value=fake_pool):
        resp = client.post('/chat', json={'message': 'hello'})
    assert resp.status_code == 200
```
evals.expected_output: A review that flags all three problems: (1) the legacy sync TestClient should be httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport; (2) the database is mocked with MagicMock instead of using a real test DB with transactional rollback; (3) the LLM is stubbed over HTTP with respx instead of faking the client at the dependency boundary. Should also note SSE responses must be consumed with the read_sse_events helper, not via resp.status_code alone.

assertions.description: Flags the legacy TestClient.
Recommends httpx AsyncClient with ASGITransport.
Flags mocking the database.
Recommends a real test DB with transactional rollback instead.
Flags stubbing the LLM over HTTP.
Recommends faking the LLM at the SDK/dependency boundary.
Review identifies all three anti-patterns (legacy TestClient, DB mocking, HTTP-level LLM stubbing) as distinct problems and proposes the correct alternative for each.
